Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a standard source URL pointing to a versioned zip archive hosted on the official project domain (files.ax86.net). The `sha256sums` array contains a valid checksum, indicating trust in the integrity of the downloaded file. There is no `pkgver()` function defined, so sourcing the PKGBUILD will not execute any dynamic version calculation. The top-level scope contains no command substitutions or immediate-execution code that would run during sourcing. The `source` array uses a direct HTTPS URL with a pinned version, which is safe for `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`. No network requests, file operations, or code executions occur outside of the declared source download during this phase. The rest of the PKGBUILD (e.g., `package()`) is irrelevant for this narrow gate as it is not executed.
</details>
<summary>
No dangerous code in sourcing or source fetch
</summary>
</security_assessment>

LLM auditresponse for terminus-font-ttf.install:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The `terminus-font-ttf.install` file contains a single `post_install()` function that outputs a message informing the user that font files have been installed into `/usr/share/fonts/TTF/`. The function uses `echo` to display this message and performs no other operations. There are no network requests, file manipulations beyond standard font installation, system modifications, or execution of external commands. This behavior is consistent with standard AUR package post-installation messaging and poses no security risk.
</details>
<summary>
Innocuous post-install informational message only</summary>
</security_assessment>

[2/3] Reviewing PKGBUILD...
+ Reviewed terminus-font-ttf.install. Status: SAFE -- Innocuous post-install informational message only
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD file defines a standard Arch Linux package for the Terminus TTF font. It downloads a versioned zip archive from the declared upstream URL, `https://files.ax86.net/terminus-ttf`, which matches the package's official source. The source is a static release (not a VCS such as git), and the provided `sha256sums` entry ensures integrity verification of the downloaded file. The package function extracts and installs `.ttf` font files into the standard font directory and places the license in the appropriate location, both standard and expected behaviors for a font package.

There are no suspicious operations such as network requests during build/install, use of `eval`, `base64`, or obfuscated commands. The script performs only straightforward file copying and renaming using predictable patterns. The `install` command is used with safe permissions (644), and all operations occur within the build environment (`srcdir`, `pkgdir`). The maintainer's contact and upstream URL appear legitimate and consistent with the project's known hosting.

The dependencies (`fontconfig`, `xorg-mkfontdir`) are typical for font packages and are used to ensure proper system integration. The accompanying `.install` script (not shown) is referenced but does not appear to contain any inline code in this file. Given the transparency of the source, proper checksums, and lack of dangerous or unexpected behavior, this package poses no security risk.
</details>
<summary>
Standard font package with verified source and no malicious code
</summary>
</security_assessment>

[3/3] Reviewing ...

LLM auditresponse for COPYING:
<security_assassessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The file provided is the COPYING file for the terminus-font-ttf package, containing the SIL Open Font License (OFL) v1.1. It includes the full text of the license, which governs the distribution and modification of the Terminus TTF font. The license is a recognized open-source license approved by both the Open Source Initiative (OSI) and the Free Software Foundation (FSF), commonly used for font software.

There is no executable code, scripts, network calls, or file operations present in this file. The content consists solely of copyright notices and license terms. While the URL http://scripts.sil.org/OFL is included, it links to an official and legitimate source for the license and its FAQ, which is standard practice. No obfuscated content, malicious behavior, or supply-chain risks are present.

This file complies with standard AUR packaging practices for open-source fonts. It clearly states licensing terms, restrictions on reserved font names, and redistribution conditions—all expected and appropriate for a package like this.
</details>
<summary>
License file with no executable code or malicious content</summary>
</security_assessment>

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD only contains static variable assignments in its global scope (pkgname, pkgver, etc.) and a `source` array pointing to an HTTP URL with a valid SHA256 checksum. There is no `pkgver()` function defined that could execute code during sourcing. The `package()` function is present but will not be executed by `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` or `makepkg --printsrcinfo`. Fetching the source via HTTP with integrity verification via checksum is a standard practice and does not constitute an immediate risk. No malicious code (e.g., command substitution, external downloads, data exfiltration) runs during the sourcing step.
</details>
<summary>No malicious code executes during source fetch or PKGBUILD sourcing.</summary>
</security_assessment>

<details>
The PKGBUILD is a straightforward packaging script for the Terminus Font TTF package. It downloads a zip file from the official upstream source (`https://files.ax86.net/terminus-ttf/files/`), validates it with a hardcoded SHA-256 checksum, and installs the font files and license into the package directory. No suspicious commands, obfuscated code, unexpected network requests, or attempts to exfiltrate data are present. The packaging follows standard Arch Linux practices. The `install` variable references an external file (`terminus-font-ttf.install`), but that file is not part of this audit; the PKGBUILD itself contains no malicious content.
</details>
<summary>Clean PKGBUILD; standard font packaging with verified checksum.</summary>
